customer_vision: Contoso Sports wants D365 to manage their 500+ media rights partnerships and sponsorship pipeline.
business_objectives: D365 Sales live for 200 sales users
Media rights contract tracking in D365
success_criteria: Full Salesforce migration with zero data loss
risks: Complex many-to-many relationship model for media rights — may require custom data model
pitfalls: Client underwent a leadership change — new CRO has different priorities
key_decisions: Custom media rights entity model in D365
PowerApps portal for partner self-service
delivery_notes: Leadership change is the key risk. Secure commitment from new CRO in Week 1.

customer_vision: Margie's CEO wants Copilot to help consultants book 20% more travel per day without adding headcount. Very clear ROI metric.
business_objectives: 3,500 consultants active on Copilot within 60 days of go-live
Measurable 15%+ booking productivity improvement
success_criteria: Productivity measurement dashboard live by Day 30 post go-live
risks: Microsoft 365 licensing not fully compliant — some users on E1 (not eligible for Copilot)
pitfalls: Client's IT team is 2 people — limited bandwidth for tenant configuration support
key_decisions: Custom travel planning prompt library as differentiator — Copilot Studio for custom agents
delivery_notes: License remediation must happen before deployment. IT team bandwidth is the constraint — give them a very detailed pre-work checklist.

customer_vision: Lucerne Publishing was the victim of a phishing attack that cost €2M. The board mandated Zero Trust within 18 months. CFO controls budget tightly.
business_objectives: Zero Trust architecture fully operational across all 5,000 users
Phishing simulation failure rate < 5%
success_criteria: Conditional Access policies blocking 99.9% of unauthorized access attempts
risks: Legacy identity infrastructure in 3 acquired companies not yet integrated into Entra ID
pitfalls: CFO put the project on hold once already due to budget — risk of repeat
key_decisions: Conditional Access over legacy VPN — aligns with remote-first workforce
delivery_notes: The project was put on hold once. Budget sensitivity is real — track spend weekly and flag any risk of overrun immediately. The CFO (Stefan Gruber) requires monthly budget reports.

customer_vision: VanArsdel's General Counsel drove this initiative after seeing a competitor use AI for contract review. The CIO is supportive but cautious on data privacy.
business_objectives: 8,000 Copilot users active and productive by go-live + 60 days
Legal contract review agent in production
success_criteria: Copilot usage rate > 60% at 90 days
Legal agent reduces contract review time by 50%
risks: Legal data privacy requirements restrict which documents can be processed by AI
pitfalls: Legal team is skeptical — contract review agent needs validation by external counsel
key_decisions: Copilot with SharePoint Restricted Content Discoverability for legal docs
Legal agent on private Azure OpenAI deployment (no public endpoint)
delivery_notes: Legal team skepticism is the cultural risk. Involve external counsel early in legal agent validation. Copilot adoption needs executive air cover — get CIO to do a launch video.

customer_vision: Erik Munson (owner-operator) wants real-time production and sales visibility on his tablet. Very practical vision — no over-engineering.
business_objectives: Real-time production dashboard for plant floor
CFO financial dashboard replacing 40 Excel reports
success_criteria: Production KPIs refreshing < 5 minutes
CFO signs off on data accuracy vs. GL
risks: MES system has no API — requires ODBC connector
pitfalls: Client has very limited IT staff — 2 people total
key_decisions: Fabric Direct Lake for production data — no data warehouse layer needed
Mobile-first Power BI design
delivery_notes: Keep it simple. Erik wants a tablet dashboard, not an enterprise analytics program. Scope creep is the #1 risk — say no to anything not in the SOW.

customer_vision: Trey Research wants a single platform to manage the full client lifecycle — from prospect to delivery. Salesforce replacement.
business_objectives: Full Salesforce migration to D365 Sales
Customer Service omnichannel live for 200 agents
success_criteria: All Salesforce data migrated with zero data loss validation
Customer Service deflection rate > 30% in 90 days
risks: Complex Salesforce data model — migration may require custom ETL
pitfalls: Client underestimated training effort for 200 CS agents
key_decisions: D365 Customer Service over ServiceNow — existing Office 365 footprint
delivery_notes: Training is the success factor. Salesforce migration is technically straightforward but the CS agent adoption needs a dedicated change program.

customer_vision: Lamna needs to pass a HIPAA audit in Q3 2026. The cloud foundation is the enabler. Very compliance-driven engagement.
business_objectives: HIPAA-compliant Azure environment operational
Entra ID fully deployed with MFA and PIM
success_criteria: HIPAA policy initiative showing 95%+ compliance in Defender for Cloud
PHI data classified and protected in Purview
risks: Legacy on-premises AD has 12 years of accumulated technical debt
pitfalls: Previous cloud vendor was AWS — some stakeholder reluctance toward Azure
key_decisions: Defender for Cloud HIPAA initiative over custom policy
Entra ID cloud-only for new accounts; hybrid for legacy
delivery_notes: HIPAA audit is the North Star. Every decision should be framed around audit readiness.

customer_vision: Datum's CEO publicly committed at their annual conference to 'AI-first operations by 2027.' This platform is the flagship initiative. High visibility internally and externally.
business_objectives: 3 production AI agents live by Q4 2026
AI governance framework adopted across all business units
50% reduction in HR onboarding processing time
success_criteria: HR agent handles 80%+ of onboarding queries without human escalation
Responsible AI review passed by external auditor
risks: AI model accuracy for procurement approvals must meet legal threshold — not yet defined
Data privacy requirements for HR agent (EU GDPR)
pitfalls: CEO announcements have created unrealistic timeline expectations internally — delivery team must manage this
Procurement data is not clean — will impact agent training
key_decisions: Semantic Kernel over LangChain — client's .NET stack
Azure OpenAI GPT-4o as foundation model
Start with HR agent as pilot — lower risk, measurable outcomes
delivery_notes: Manage expectations carefully — the CEO has been public about this. Start with HR agent. Do not start procurement agent until HR agent is in production and outcomes validated.

customer_vision: City Power & Light operates critical infrastructure. A ransomware incident at a peer utility last year triggered a board mandate for enterprise SOC capability. NERC CIP compliance is a regulatory requirement.
business_objectives: Sentinel deployed across IT and OT/ICS environments
NERC CIP compliance analytics running
24x7 SOC with 4-analyst team operational
success_criteria: Zero undetected incidents > 15 minutes in OT environment
NERC CIP audit passed with Sentinel evidence package
risks: OT/ICS environment has air-gapped segments — Sentinel connectivity requires special architecture
NERC CIP requires evidence of 6-month monitoring history before audit
pitfalls: Previous security tool (QRadar) has complex existing rules — migration effort underestimated
Regulatory timeline is fixed — no slip allowed
key_decisions: Sentinel over QRadar — native Microsoft integration and lower TCO
Defender for IoT for OT/ICS visibility
delivery_notes: The NERC CIP audit is scheduled for January 2027 — this is immovable. OT environment connectivity requires working with their SCADA team (separate from IT). Get SCADA team alignment in Week 1.

customer_vision: Wingtip CTO wants to demonstrate a cloud-native portfolio to the board by Q4 2026 to support their IPO narrative. 35 applications is aggressive for 12 months.
business_objectives: 35 applications containerized and running on AKS by December 2026
CI/CD pipelines for all applications
Zero on-premises application footprint
success_criteria: All 35 apps passing automated integration tests in AKS
P95 response time ≤ pre-migration baseline
risks: Application count is 35 — team capacity may not support all in scope
Some apps have undocumented dependencies
pitfalls: App #17 (inventory) has a mainframe dependency that was NOT disclosed during discovery — material scope risk
Dev team has limited Kubernetes experience
key_decisions: AKS over App Service — future scalability requirements
GitHub Actions for CI/CD — client has existing GitHub Enterprise
delivery_notes: App #17 inventory system has an undocumented AS/400 mainframe dependency. This is a blocker and needs an architecture decision before containerization. Escalate immediately in kickoff.

customer_vision: Blue Yonder wants to be the AI-powered supply chain platform of record for Asia-Pacific. This Fabric implementation is the data foundation for their AI roadmap.
business_objectives: Unified data platform live with all 6 legacy sources migrated
Executive Power BI suite replacing 40 disparate reports
Purview data catalog with 80%+ asset coverage
success_criteria: OneLake ingesting all 6 legacy data sources
Power BI certified dataset adopted by 200+ analysts
risks: Data quality issues in legacy ERP will delay pipeline development
Only 1 internal data engineer — key person risk
pitfalls: Client previously tried a Databricks implementation — abandoned after 6 months due to skills gap
Stakeholder alignment on data ownership is weak
key_decisions: OneLake over Synapse Analytics — better Fabric native integration
Purview for governance from day 1, not as an afterthought
delivery_notes: Legacy ERP data quality is worse than disclosed during pre-sales. Build in a 3-week data profiling sprint before any pipeline work. The 1 internal data engineer (Raj Patel) is excellent — leverage him heavily.

customer_vision: Southridge CEO committed to board that D365 will be live for fiscal year close in March 2028. This is non-negotiable. CFO is the day-to-day sponsor and is very hands-on.
business_objectives: Go-live on D365 F&O by March 2028 for fiscal year close
Eliminate PeopleSoft by end of 2027
Achieve real-time financial visibility across 12 subsidiaries
success_criteria: Parallel run: D365 and PeopleSoft for Q1 2028 close
Clean data migration validated by external auditor
risks: Complex media rights revenue recognition requirements may require ISV
Only 3 internal D365-trained staff
pitfalls: Client attempted D365 F&O once before with a different SI — project was cancelled after 8 months
Finance team is resistant to change — heavy change management needed
key_decisions: Custom media rights module to be built on D365 extensibility framework
Phase 1: Finance only; Phase 2: SCM; Phase 3: Media Rights
delivery_notes: Day 1 priority: run design workshops with CFO (Patricia Lim) before any configuration. Previous SI failure was caused by skipping design. Change management is critical — budget has been allocated.

customer_vision: Proseware was breached 18 months ago. The CISO has board mandate to achieve enterprise-class security operations this fiscal year. Political capital is high.
business_objectives: Deploy Sentinel with full SIEM coverage
MTTD < 15 min, MTTR < 4 hours for critical incidents
Eliminate Splunk and reduce licensing 40%
success_criteria: 100% critical assets covered by Defender for Endpoint
40+ Sentinel connectors operational
SOC team of 4 analysts fully trained
risks: Splunk decommission depends on 3rd-party SLA
2 of 4 SOC analyst headcount still unfilled
pitfalls: CISO and IT Ops Director have conflicting SOC ownership views
Previous integrator left connectors undocumented
key_decisions: Sentinel over Splunk Cloud — cost and native MS integration
Managed SOAR playbooks via Logic Apps
delivery_notes: Weekly delivery review with CISO (Tom Bradley) every Tuesday 9am EST. Do NOT discuss Splunk deprecation in joint calls — politically sensitive.

customer_vision: Adatum wants to be fully cloud-native within 36 months. This engagement is the strategic foundation — the CTO views it as a career-defining transformation.
business_objectives: Establish a secure, compliant Azure Landing Zone by August 2026
Enable self-service workload onboarding for 10 application teams
Achieve 25% infrastructure cost reduction vs. on-premises in year 1
success_criteria: Landing Zone passes Microsoft Well-Architected Framework review
First 5 workloads migrated by project close
risks: Only 1 internal Azure architect — key-person risk
ExpressRoute provisioning lead times (6–8 weeks)
pitfalls: Client underestimated policy remediation scope — budget pressure likely in Phase 2
Messy existing tenant from prior POC
key_decisions: Hub-spoke over Azure Virtual WAN — client prefers control
Custom Policy-as-Code (Bicep) not Azure Blueprints
delivery_notes: Start with 2-week discovery sprint to audit existing tenant. CTO (Lisa Park) has weekly 30-min check-ins — do not miss these.
